message:
feat(tui/styles): add reduced motion accessibility support (ntm-952.5)

Implement NTM_REDUCE_MOTION environment variable support for users who
prefer reduced motion due to vestibular disorders or personal preference.
This is a key accessibility feature for the Premium UX Polish epic.

Changes to styles.go:
- Add reducedMotionEnabled() helper that checks NTM_REDUCE_MOTION env var
- Accepts truthy values: "1", "true", "yes", "on" (case-insensitive)
- Shimmer(): returns static GradientText when reduced motion enabled
- Pulse(): returns base color unchanged when reduced motion enabled

Behavior when NTM_REDUCE_MOTION=1:
- Shimmer animations become static gradient text (still visually distinct)
- Pulse effects return the base color (no brightness oscillation)
- All other styling preserved (gradients, glows, colors)

Design rationale:
- Users with motion sensitivity can disable animations globally
- Effects degrade to visually-equivalent static representations
- No functionality lost - only animation timing removed
- Follows WCAG reduced motion guidelines

Test coverage added:
- TestShimmer: verifies stable output under reduced motion
- TestPulse: verifies base color returned under reduced motion
- Tests use t.Setenv for clean isolation

Part of the Accessibility sub-epic (ntm-952.5) ensuring NTM is usable
in all terminal environments and for users with varying needs.
